business.headline: Why bank lockers are hard to get and what you can do insteadThis is the third part of Lock & Key, a series that looks at how India's bank locker system works, the risks involved, and what customers need to know. After examining safety and insurance in the first two parts, this story focusses on a new problem – access.

World economy in serious trouble, India vulnerable: Raghuram Rajan on oil crisisThe former RBI Govenor said a 15–20% global energy disruption would require sharp demand cuts, pushing oil to $150–$200 per barrel, risking severe economic stress if the conflict continues another month.

Oil could hit $150-$200 per barrel if Iran war drags on: Raghuram RajanFormer RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan warned that if the US-Israeli war against Iran continues beyond a month, oil prices could surge to $150-$200 per barrel, potentially triggering a global recession.

Premium petrol prices up by Rs 2, no change in normal petrol, diesel ratesIndian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have increased premium petrol prices by ₹2 per litre, with IOCL XP95 now priced at ₹101.80 per litre. The hike affects premium petrol used mostly in performance vehicles, while regular petrol prices remain unchanged.
Sensex ends 326 points higher, Nifty above 23,100; HDFC Bank falls 2%The S&P BSE Sensex added 325.72 points to end at 74,532.96, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 112.35 points to end at 23,114.50.

Zomato hikes food delivery platform fee to Rs 14.90 from Rs 12.50 per orderThe platform fee now stands at Rs 14.90 per order on a pre-GST basis, up from Rs 12.50 earlier. This marks another round of increase, with the last hike having taken place in September 2025.

Flipkart CFO Sriram Venkataraman to step down ahead of planned IPOVenkataraman joined Flipkart in 2015 and has been part of the company's leadership team through a period of growth and restructuring. During his tenure, he helped strengthen the company's financial operations.

Premium petrol prices hiked by Rs 2 per litre; regular fuel unchangedThe hike applies to high-octane fuels such as Indian Oil's XP95 and Hindustan Petroleum's Power petrol, based on dealer inputs from select cities.

Is the 20-minute neighbourhood the future of urban living?Urban planners across global cities increasingly speak of the "20-minute neighbourhood", a model where residents can access most aspects of daily life within roughly twenty minutes.

Buying insurance online? 8 in 10 users say it can lead to problemsA nationwide survey has found that more than 80% of users faced issues such as hidden charges, difficulty with cancelling policies, forced data sharing and misleading pricing while using online insurance platforms.

Gold prices today: Why are rates rising after a steep fall and should you buy?Gold prices are edging higher after a sharp fall, but the trend remains uncertain as global cues and a strong dollar continue to drive volatility.

HDFC Bank share price extends fall, down 2% after sharp sell-offThe stock slipped 2.19% to Rs 782.15 on the BSE at 10:35 am, adding to losses after it had plunged more than 5% in the previous session, marking one of its sharpest declines in recent months.

Rupee hits all-time low, slips past Rs 93 mark against US dollarThe rupee weakened to Rs 93.15 against the US dollar in early trade, extending recent losses amid rising geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today? 3 key reasonsThe rebound comes as easing oil prices, positive global cues, strong buying in IT stocks, and broad-based buying after Thursday's sharp fall lifted investor sentiment.

Is Dubai's luxury housing boom starting to crack as Gulf conflict escalates?Dubai's property market faces a key test as tensions rise, sentiment weakens, and luxury villa deals come under pressure, with the outlook hinging on how demand and geopolitical risks evolve.
Sensex jumps 800 points, Nifty above 23,200; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 808.29 points to 75,015.53, while the NSE Nifty50 added 257.45 points to 23,259.60 as of 9:26 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,250.5 at around 08:24 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Thursday's close of 23,002.15.
Ports power India's growth: Why sea trade matters more than everAcross the country, cities that have built strong linkages between ports and industry have often emerged as important economic hubs.
US issues fresh 30-day sanctions waiver for purchase of Russian oil at seaThe license, which expires on April 11, replaces and supersedes a similar 30-day sanctions waiver issued on March 12.

US says it may lift sanctions on Iranian oil stranded in tankers to calm pricesUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the addition of sanctioned Iranian oil into global supplies would help keep oil prices down for the next 10 to 14 days. Oil prices have been above $100 per barrel for much of the past two weeks as Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz to shipping and has attacked tankers.

entertainment.headline: SS Rajamouli praises Ranveer Singh in Dhurandhar 2, calls it a masterclass in acting SS Rajamouli praised Dhurandhar: The Revenge, saying it is better than the original and appreciating its strong emotions and storytelling.

Viral: Deepika Padukone enjoys Rishab Rikhiram's concert with Ranveer Singh's family Deepika Padukone attended Rishab Rikhiram Sharma's Mumbai concert, where she was seen enjoying the performance with Ranveer Singh's sister Ritika Bhavnani and mother Anju Bhavnani.

Beyond the lead cast: 7 unsung heroes of Dhurandhar 2 who deserve more attention  In an industry driven by star power and visibility, Dhurandhar: The Revenge proves that the right casting can turn overlooked actors into the film's most lasting impact.

Rakesh Roshan calls Dhurandhar 2 a game-changer: Reminder to reset cinema Rakesh Roshan has praised Dhurandhar: The Revenge, calling it a game-changer and a cinematic milestone that redefines storytelling in Indian cinema.

Pankaj Tripathi, Jaideep Ahlawat, Vijay Varma: Indian OTT builds its star system OTT in India started out as a launchpad for fresh talent and ideas. With platforms mostly chasing subscribers now, the space seems more interested in creating a star system of its own that fetches assured returns.

Ram Charan calls Dhurandhar 2 remarkable, praises Ranveer Singh's performance Ram Charan has praised Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, calling Aditya Dhar's direction remarkable and Ranveer Singh's performance phenomenal.

Dhurandhar 2: How Jaskirat Singh Rangi's story shapes heart of the film Dhurandhar: The Revenge was set to take a superb opening at the box office simply because of the expectations set by the first part. A major reason why the sequel exceeds these expectations is the emotional depth Jaskirat Singh Rangi's story adds to Hamza's fire.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 2: Rs 200 crore milestone done, Eid bonanza ahead Dhurandhar: The Revenge has entered the Rs 200 crore club in just two days at the box office, but the real surge is yet to begin as the weekend and Eid festivities kick in. The film's winning run has only just begun.

Akshay Kumar pays emotional tribute to Chuck Norris: Shaped a part of me Akshay Kumar paid tribute to late actor Chuck Norris, reflecting on how watching the Hollywood legend's "effortless command" on screen profoundly shaped his own martial arts journey.

Chuck Norris was America's Rajinikanth Chuck Norris, legendary martial artist and pop culture icon, passed away at 86, leaving behind a larger-than-life legacy shaped as much by his films as by the global phenomenon of "Chuck Norris facts."

From reel to reality: Rashmika and Vijay host little fan at Hyderabad home. Watch Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deveraonda hosted a young fan and her family at their Hyderabad home. The girl had expressed a wish to attend the couple's wedding in a viral video.

Legendary action star Chuck Norris dies at 86 Legendary Hollywood action star of Walker Texas Ranger fame, Chuck Norris, died at the age of 86. He featured in iconic films, such as Lone Wolf McQuade (1983), Code of Silence (1985) and Bruce Lee's The Way of the Dragon (1972).

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 eyes Rs 2000 crore run, trade calls it a 'tsunami' Dhurandhar 2 created box office history with huge advance bookings and massive footfall on day 1. Trade analysts compare its craze to Sholay and predict a massive weekend.

Dhurandhar 2 bash: Ranveer grooves to Fa9la, Saumya Tandon recreates slap scene Several videos from the Dhurandhar 2 bash have gone viral on social media. Ranveer Singh grooved to the Fa9la song and recreated a slap scene with Saumya Tandon. Ayesha Khan, too, shared behind-the-scenes photos from the party.

Dhurandhar: How Aditya Dhar made India sit in theatres for 7 hours in reels era In an age of dwindling attention span, director Aditya Dhar has achieved the remarkable feat of holding Indian audiences captive for over seven hours across two films. His Dhurandhar series blends history and fiction to create a compelling cinematic universe.

Prakash Raj takes jibe at Allu Arjun, Jr NTR over Dhurandhar 2 praise Actor Prakash Raj has questioned the increasing praise for Dhurandhar 2 from South stars such as Allu Arjun, Ram Charan and Jr NTR. He took a subtle dig at the espionage thriller on social media.

Dhurandhar 2 in Pakistan: Viral video claims pirated film being watched in Lahore Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 is allegedly drawing attention in Pakistan despite a ban on Indian films.  A viral video has surfaced online showing a man watching a pirated version of the film in Lahore.

Aahana Kumra cheers for Danish Pandor after Dhurandhar 2 success: Happiest for him Aahana Kumra celebrated the success of Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, praising the film's director and cast. Her special mention of Danish Pandor caught significant attention amid rumours of their relationship.

Dhurandhar 2 seizes box office without a fight: Every record it broke so far Dhurandhar: The Revenge has turned its opening day into a full-blown box office event, delivering numbers that place it among the biggest in Indian cinema. With records already shattered and momentum building fast, the film's run is only just beginning.

general news.headline: Two more India-bound LPG tankers to cross war-hit Strait of HormuzWith hundreds of vessels anchored and crude flows stalled, two Indian LPG tankers prepare to sail through Hormuz.

How Rahul Gandhi's displeasure with Kerala poll list led to marathon midnight meetThe Congress has announced candidates for 92 seats so far in Kerala. India Today has exclusively learnt that Leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, was unhappy with the method of choosing candidates and that led to a marathon meeting recently.

Has Israel gone rogue on the US? Israel and the US started the Iran war together. But the killing of Larijani and the striking of the South Pars gas field by Israel are being seen as "unilateral" acts. Has Israel gone rogue?
Rare March fog shrouds Delhi on Eid: What's behind it?The dense cover of fog that covered Delhi-NCR early Saturday was unseasonal. What caused it?

Prakash Raj takes jibe at Allu Arjun, Jr NTR over Dhurandhar 2 praiseActor Prakash Raj has questioned the increasing praise for Dhurandhar 2 from South stars such as Allu Arjun, Ram Charan and Jr NTR. He took a subtle dig at the espionage thriller on social media.

Trump considers winding down Iran war, says others must guard Strait of HormuzPresident Donald Trump said the US is close to meeting its Iran war objectives, signalled a possible drawdown of military efforts, and urged other nations to take responsibility for securing the Strait of Hormuz.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 2: Rs 200 crore milestone done, Eid bonanza aheadDhurandhar: The Revenge has entered the Rs 200 crore club in just two days at the box office, but the real surge is yet to begin as the weekend and Eid festivities kick in. The film's winning run has only just begun.

Beat report: Mumbai's Mithi river isn't dirty, it's a system in decayWith no contractors stepping forward to clean the Mithi river and the monsoon fast approaching, Mumbai faces rising flood risks, exposing systemic failures, administrative gaps and public neglect that continue to leave the city vulnerable year after year.

Jesus, Genghis Khan and Israel that Netanyahu has madeOn Thursday night, Netanyahu said, "Jesus Christ has no advantage over Genghis Khan. Because if you are strong enough, ruthless enough, powerful enough, evil will overcome good. Aggression will overcome moderation."

I don't want a ceasefire: Trump says US is 'obliterating' Iran as war rages onTrump ruled out a ceasefire with Iran, saying the US will continue military operations despite openness to dialogue, as he claimed Tehran's military capabilities have been largely destroyed.

Fear, threats, fake rituals to exploit women: Inside Ashok Kharat's web of coercionPolice said he manipulated a 35-year-old married woman through rituals and claims of divine powers, drugged her with intoxicants, hypnotised her, and repeatedly assaulted her.

Live: Israeli forces claim 20+ Hezbollah terrorists killed in 24 hours in LebanonUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The United States is reinforcing its Middle East presence, sending three additional warships and about 2,500 Marines, bringing troop levels to roughly 50,000. The Pentagon is seeking $200 billion in war funding as national debt reaches $39 trillion. Fighting has intensified, with Iran launching strikes on Israel and Gulf energy sites while Israel targets Tehran. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claimed Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities are crippled, though damage remains unclear. Civilian tolls are mounting across the region, with thousands killed or displaced in Iran, Lebanon and beyond, while energy infrastructure attacks have driven global oil and gas prices sharply higher. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.
Jayalalithaa's Crown: Schemes & Shadow of Sister Sasikala | Part 35

Truth is harsh: Ex-J&K DGP defends Dhurandhar 2 amid propaganda rowFormer J&K DGP SP Vaid defended Dhurandhar 2 as based on truth, while Congress criticised it as BJP propaganda, sparking a political row despite the film's strong box office performance.

Why Iran's killer Shahed drones have moped-like soundThe Shahed-136 drone has emerged as Tehran's weapon of choice in the ongoing war in the Middle East. It was used by Russia in the Ukraine war. The Ukrainians call the Shahed-136 "a moped" because of the sound it makes. Here's more about Shahed-136's unique journey and the reason behind the distinctive sound.

Dhurandhar: How Aditya Dhar made India sit in theatres for 7 hours in reels eraIn an age of dwindling attention span, director Aditya Dhar has achieved the remarkable feat of holding Indian audiences captive for over seven hours across two films. His Dhurandhar series blends history and fiction to create a compelling cinematic universe.

Pankaj Tripathi, Jaideep Ahlawat, Vijay Varma: Indian OTT builds its star systemOTT in India started out as a launchpad for fresh talent and ideas. With platforms mostly chasing subscribers now, the space seems more interested in creating a star system of its own that fetches assured returns.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Dhurandhar 2: How Jaskirat Singh Rangi's story shapes heart of the filmDhurandhar: The Revenge was set to take a superb opening at the box office simply because of the expectations set by the first part. A major reason why the sequel exceeds these expectations is the emotional depth Jaskirat Singh Rangi's story adds to Hamza's fire.

Delhi's quiet football comeback: 6,732 reasons for the capital to believe againDelhi waited seven years for football to come home, and when it finally did, the response was not loud or overwhelming. But it was present, it was honest, and it quietly suggested something had begun again.

India's corporate culture talks mental health, but rewards burnout insteadIndia's corporate success story hides a growing mental health crisis, where burnout, stigma, and pressure remain deeply embedded in workplace culture, even as employees increasingly demand change and companies struggle to keep up.

Son makes mother meet her childhood crush in epic surprise. Her happy tears say it allA man surprised his mother by arranging a meeting with her childhood crush Jonty Rhodes, capturing her emotional reaction in a heartwarming video that went viral online.

Fear limits testimonies as probe widens in Nashik godman Ashok Kharat rape caseFear, influence and silence shape the unfolding Ashok Kharat abuse case in Maharashtra, as villagers, officials and even victims hesitate to speak, while investigators uncover disturbing evidence and a vast network of connections.

Bad day at work? Gen Z is using dopamine dressing to fix it with fashionIn an era of workplace monotony, Gen Z is turning to bright colours and dopamine dressing to boost their mood and confidence while navigating burnou, deadlines, and the silent pressures of professionalism.

sports.headline: India make Belfast return after 19 years as IRE T20I series schedule released India are all set to make a return to Belfast as the schedule for their upcoming T20I series against Ireland was confirmed by the BCCI on Saturday, March 21. India will be returning to the city after a gap of 19 years.

Delhi's quiet football comeback: 6,732 reasons for the capital to believe again Delhi waited seven years for football to come home, and when it finally did, the response was not loud or overwhelming. But it was present, it was honest, and it quietly suggested something had begun again.

Raghuvanshi hits statement hundred in KKR intra-squad match, Rahane flops Angkrish Raghuvanshi produced a statement knock during KKR's intra-squad match ahead of the IPL 2026 season, scoring an unbeaten 103 off just 55 balls. While Raghuvanshi impressed, Ajinkya Rahane and Umran Malik struggled to find early form.

Maguire on red card during Manchester United vs Bournemouth: That's football today Harry Maguire slammed the controversial call made by Stuart Attwell to give him a red card during Manchester United's 2-2 draw against Bournemouth on Friday, March 20. United felt that they should have got a penalty earlier for a foul on Amad Diallo.

Cristiano Ronaldo set to miss Portugal friendlies ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 Cristiano Ronaldo misses Portugal's March friendlies with a hamstring issue, but the bigger focus remains his fitness for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as Portugal manage their captain's workload carefully.

15-year-old Rwanda batter scripts history with Women's T20I century on debut Rwanda's Fanny Utagushimaninde became the youngest T20I centurion at 15, scoring 111 on debut against Ghana. Her knock powered Rwanda to a commanding 122-run victory in the Nigeria Invitational Women's T20I Tournament.

Jasprit Bumrah of KKR: Vaibhav Arora outlines his goal for IPL 2026 season Fast bowler Vaibhav Arora aims to serve the Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026 the way Jasprit Bumrah serves the Indian team. The speedster has taken 33 wickets for KKR from 27 matches.

KKR have powerful, world-class players, along with experience and youth: Shane Watson IPL 2026: In a press conference facilitated by JioStar on Friday, assistant coach Shane Watson said that the Kolkata Knight Riders have a strong squad at their disposal with a blend of youth and experience.

Sayali Satghare relishes special 2026 journey: From WPL glory to Perth Test debut Sayali Satghare reflected on a dream few months that saw her go from unsold in the 2026 WPL auction to winning the title with RCB and making her India Test debut at the WACA in Perth.

Wheatley exits as Audi's team principal amid rumours of Aston Martin move F1: Jonathan Wheatley has left Audi F1 with immediate effect as speculation grows over a move to struggling Aston Martin, with Mattia Binotto stepping up as the new team principal.

Shreyas Iyer recalls recovery journey after freak injury vs Australia: Lost 7 kilos Shreyas Iyer is back after a tough injury layoff and ready to lead Punjab Kings again. The PBKS skipper now has his sights set on going one step further in IPL 2026.

Italy turn to Federico Chiesa after 2-year absence for FIFA World Cup 2026 playoffsFIFA World Cup 2026: Federico Chiesa returns to the Italy squad for the first time in two years as Gattuso's side looks to avoid a third consecutive World Cup absence in the upcoming playoffs.

Coolest thing ever: French wildcard Moise Kouame reacts to Novak Djokovic's DMMiami Open: 17-year-old Moise Kouame was left speechless after receiving a congratulatory DM from idol Novak Djokovic, calling it the 'coolest thing ever' after beating Zachary Svajda in the opening round.

No Pathirana till mid-April, figuring out Harshit replacement: KKR head coachIPL 2026: KKR are scrambling to figure out a Harshit Rana replacement while Matheesha Pathirana remains weeks away from fitness. Can they patch up their pace crisis before IPL 2026 gets underway?

Cricket's impossible schedule: Injuries taking joy out of big tournamentsThe IPL season is upon us, yet the focus is completely on teams scrambling to replace injured players. With some of the stars managing their workload and delaying their arrival, the excitement has been watered down. Is the packed cricket schedule to blame for this?

Laxman Sivaramakrishnan cites racism behind commentary retirementLaxman Sivaramakrishnan has stepped away from BCCI commentary after over two decades. The former India spinner has made direct allegations of racism, putting the spotlight on how broadcast roles are assigned.

Iga Swiatek in disbelief after shock Miami Open defeat: Tennis feels complicatedMiami Open 2026: World No.3 Iga Swiatek slumped to a shock defeat against fellow Pole Magda Linette, who sealed the upset in 2 hours and 10 minutes.

KKR sweat on combination as team tries to find a spot for Cameron GreenIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' biggest challenge in the IPL 2026 is to figure out what their batting order is going to look like. KKR have an abundance of opening options in their batting line-up and will need to figure out who to fit down the order.

Mainoo, Maguire return as Tuchel names England friendlies squadKobbie Mainoo and Harry Maguire are back in the England squad at a crucial time. Thomas Tuchel's latest selection gives the Manchester United duo a fresh chance ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

India Women set for landmark Test return in South Africa after 24 yearsIndia Women will return to Test cricket in South Africa after 24 years as part of a tour that includes three ODIs and a one-off Test in December, Cricket South Africa announced on Friday.

Tom Latham, bowlers script New Zealand's record T20I win against South AfricaNZ vs SA, 3rd T20I: Tom Latham scored 63 off 55 as New Zealand beat South Africa by eight wickets at the Eden Park in Auckland to take a 2-1 lead in the five-match series.

India has one of the best indoor stadiums in South Asia: SumariwallaIndia is set to host its first ever World Indoor Athletics Championships in 2028. World Athletics vice president Adille Sumariwalla reacted to the announcement and said that India has one of the best indoor infrastructure in the world.

IPL 2026: How have CSK treated their big buys? A story of injury and misfortuneIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have a costly habit of big buys flopping. Can their 28.4 crore investment in youth finally break the curse in IPL 2026?

Aston Martin stand firm with Adrian Newey, dismiss speculations on rumoured exitAston Martin insist Adrian Newey remains team principal amid reports the acclaimed designer is set to be replaced by Audi's Jonathan Wheatley at the struggling Silverstone outfit.

KKR audition ex-MI and RCB pacers as Harshit Rana replacementsKolkata Knight Riders have called up a host of fast bowlers for late trials, in the hopes of replacing Harshit Rana in the IPL 2026 season. Rana was injured in February and is yet to recover.

No media: How RR plans to shield Sooryavanshi from spotlight ahead of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals will look to shield Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from media spotlight to ensure that the youngster can freely play his natural game ahead of the new season.

'Uncle' Shashi Tharoor recalls meeting 14-year-old Samson, Dhoni predictionShashi Tharoor said that he is more like an uncle to Sanju Samson and recalled meeting the wicketkeeper when he was 14 years old. Tharoor also said that he had dubbed Samson to be the next MS Dhoni right after he met him.

Best in the world? Premier League clubs handed a European reality checkLaLiga sides outclassed Premier League clubs in the Champions League Round of 16, exposing the gap between hype and execution. Spanish teams once again showed why they remained Europe's most reliable performers.

Starc joins Cummins and Hazlewood, set for delayed IPL 2026 startMitchell Starc has joined Pat Cummins and Josh Hazlewood in having a delayed start to the upcoming IPL 2026 season. The decision was made as Cricket Australia look to manage the workload of their big three pacers ahead of a packed international schedule.

India vs Ireland T20I series confirmed for June, Stirling steps down as captainIreland are all set to welcome India to their shores in June this year for a short T20I series, Cricket Ireland announced on Thursday, March 19. India will take on the Irish side ahead of their white-ball tour of England.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
